description: Specificity Gradient Benchmark — 30 seeds across 6 topic clusters (languages, web, databases, ML, systems, security) with 5 seeds each. 18 queries at 3 specificity tiers (6 broad, 6 medium, 6 narrow) testing how retrieval precision and ranking change as queries move from general to highly specific.

tierAnalysis.description: Per-tier average metrics showing how performance varies with query specificity

observations: sp-q02 (web applications) is the weakest broad query — 0.40 recall across all modes, web seeds too diverse
sp-q01 (programming languages) achieves perfect recall in vector_rerank/hybrid_rerank — language seeds cluster tightly
sp-q05 (server infrastructure) struggles in hybrid modes — BM25 noise from generic 'server' and 'cloud' terms
Medium queries consistently outperform broad queries — the sweet spot for this embedding model
sp-q12 (concurrent programming) correctly retrieves Go, JavaScript, C# with async/await
Medium queries achieve perfect MRR in vector and hybrid modes — first result always correct
Narrow queries achieve near-perfect recall and nDCG in vector and vector_rerank modes
sp-q16 (Rust ownership) correctly pinpoints sp-lang-rust as #1 with 1.0 MRR across all modes
Low precision (0.20-0.60) expected — narrow queries have fewer relevant seeds so K=5 window includes irrelevant results

weakQueries.analysis: Weakest query across all datasets. 'Web applications' is too generic — retrieves CI/CD, React, and non-web seeds. The web cluster seeds are individually specific (HTTP, REST, React, CSS, auth) but the broad query doesn't overlap enough with any of them.
Misses sp-sys-containers in vector mode. 'Cloud operations' is vague — Go language leaks in because of Kubernetes/Docker associations.

keyFindings: The specificity gradient reveals a clear 'Goldilocks zone': medium queries (0.933 recall) outperform both broad (0.792) and narrow (1.000 recall but low precision)
Vector_rerank is the best overall mode (0.919 recall, 0.905 nDCG) — reranker adds +5.0% recall over pure vector
Hybrid_rerank is the worst mode (0.803 recall, 0.826 nDCG) — BM25 noise hurts when queries use common terms across clusters
Narrow queries achieve near-perfect ranking (nDCG 0.987) — the embedding model excels at specific technical queries
Broad queries suffer from cross-cluster leakage — general terms like 'building', 'applications', 'systems' pull in irrelevant seeds
MRR is excellent across all tiers (0.944-1.000) — the system nearly always gets the top result right regardless of query specificity
